action.hypothesis: The recent `signum` LR refinements did not convert into an accepted frontier update, so the next clean question is whether the current `topk` mainline is slightly over-regularized rather than under-tuned on step size. This round keeps the exact reference backbone `topk/signum/K128/EF12/LR=1.6e-3` fixed and changes only `AUXK_ALPHA`, reducing it from `0.03125` to `0.015625` to test whether weaker auxiliary pressure improves late-stage reconstruction without destabilizing the curve.
action.summary: Run a single loss-axis test on the existing topk mainline: keep `ARCHITECTURE=topk`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, `K=128`, `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12`, and `LR=1.6e-3` fixed, and lower `AUXK_ALPHA` to `0.015625` to see whether the current recipe is over-regularized.
action.self_review: This avoids the previous policy-reject by anchoring all core axes to the current reference recipe and changing only one non-core parameter. It also follows the prescribed order better than moving K now, since loss shaping has not been isolated yet on the accepted `1.6e-3` mainline.
notes_to_memory: Round 13 was rejected because the proposed aux-loss test also inherited a non-reference LR, so the action was not single-variable relative to the current mainline reference.
Round 14 isolates `AUXK_ALPHA` on the exact accepted topk/signum/K128/EF12/LR1.6e-3 recipe.
Evaluate the run by full curve shape, especially whether lower aux pressure improves late-stage descent or causes weaker feature utilization and noisier training.
next_hypotheses: If `AUXK_ALPHA=0.015625` improves FVU with a smooth curve, continue tuning aux-loss strength on the same mainline recipe.
If lower aux weight hurts stability or gives no gain, revert to `0.03125` and treat loss shaping as mostly settled.
If aux-loss tuning is neutral, the topk K128 recipe is closer to saturation and K exploration becomes the next useful axis.

result.description: Run a single loss-axis test on the existing topk mainline: keep `ARCHITECTURE=topk`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, `K=128`, `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12`, and `LR=1.6e-3` fixed, and lower `AUXK_ALPHA` to `0.015625` to see whether the current recipe is over-regularized.
result.self_review: This avoids the previous policy-reject by anchoring all core axes to the current reference recipe and changing only one non-core parameter. It also follows the prescribed order better than moving K now, since loss shaping has not been isolated yet on the accepted `1.6e-3` mainline.
